Pour the tadka over the pachadi.
Mix gently and serve at room temperature.
A must-have side in Kerala meals.
notes: Kerala pachadi is different from North Indian raita. It's cooked with coconut paste and tempered with mustard seeds.

name: Erissery
ingredients: 1 cup raw banana or pumpkin cubed
1/2 cup red cowpeas (vanpayar) soaked and cooked
preparation: Soak and cook red cowpeas until tender.
Cook pumpkin or raw banana with turmeric and salt until soft.
Coarsely grind half the coconut with cumin seeds and red chillies.
Add the cooked cowpeas to the cooked vegetable.
Add the ground coconut paste and mix gently.
Simmer on low heat for 8-10 minutes.
Roast remaining coconut in coconut oil until golden.
Add mustard seeds and curry leaves to the roasted coconut.
Pour this over the erissery and mix.
Serve with rice as part of a Kerala meal.
notes: A traditional Kerala dish combining pumpkin/banana with beans and coconut. The roasted coconut topping is unique.

name: Poriyal
ingredients: 2 cups beans or carrot or cabbage finely chopped
preparation: Finely chop the vegetable of choice.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds.
When they splutter, add urad dal and chana dal. Fry until golden.
Add dried red chillies and curry leaves.
Add chopped onion and cook until soft.
Add the vegetable, turmeric, and salt.
Sprinkle 2 tbsp water, cover and cook on low heat for 8-10 minutes.
The vegetable should be cooked but retain crunch.
Add grated coconut and toss well.
Serve as a side dish with rice and sambhar.
notes: Tamil Nadu's everyday stir-fried vegetable dish. Similar to Kerala's thoran but with dal in the tempering.

name: Kootu Curry
ingredients: 1 cup black chickpeas or red cowpeas cooked
1 cup raw banana or yam cubed
preparation: Cook the legumes and vegetables separately until tender.
Grind coconut with cumin seeds and green chillies to a paste.
Combine cooked vegetables and legumes in a pan.
Add turmeric, salt, and the coconut paste.
Mix gently and simmer for 8-10 minutes.
Heat coconut oil, add mustard seeds.
When they splutter, add sliced shallots and curry leaves.
Fry until shallots are golden.
Pour the tadka over the kootu curry.
Serve with rice or appam.
notes: Kerala-style kootu curry is thicker than Tamil kootu. A hearty and filling dish with legumes and vegetables.

name: Keerai Masiyal
ingredients: 3 cups spinach or amaranth greens (keerai) chopped
preparation: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric and water for 2 whistles.
Separately cook keerai (greens) with a little water until wilted.
Mash the cooked greens well with a masher or ladle.
Mix the mashed greens with the cooked dal.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and cumin seeds.
When they splutter, add curry leaves, asafoetida, and green chillies.
Add chopped onion and cook until golden.
Pour this tempering into the greens-dal mixture.
Mix well and simmer for 5 minutes.
Serve with rice and rasam.
notes: A Tamil Nadu preparation of mashed greens with dal. Very nutritious and iron-rich. Perfect for everyday lunch.

name: Sundal Lunch
ingredients: 1 cup chickpeas or black-eyed peas soaked and cooked
preparation: Soak and cook the legumes until tender but firm. Drain well.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds.
When they splutter, add urad dal and fry until golden.
Add dried red chillies, curry leaves, and asafoetida.
Add green chillies and cooked legumes.
Add turmeric and salt. Toss well.
Cook for 3-4 minutes to dry out any excess moisture.
Add grated coconut and toss well.
Remove from heat immediately after adding coconut.
Serve as a protein-rich side dish for lunch with rice.
notes: Sundal is traditionally a temple and festival food but makes a great protein-rich lunch side dish.

name: Rasam Vada
ingredients: For vada: 1 cup urad dal soaked, 2 green chillies, 1 small onion chopped, curry leaves, salt
For rasam: 1/4 cup toor dal cooked, 2 tomatoes, small tamarind ball, 2 tsp rasam powder, 1/2 tsp turmeric
preparation: Soak urad dal for 4 hours. Grind to a thick paste with green chillies.
Add chopped onion, curry leaves, and salt. Mix well.
Shape into round vadas with a hole in the center.
Deep fry until golden and crispy. Drain and set aside.
For rasam: Extract tamarind juice. Mash cooked toor dal.
Boil tomatoes with tamarind juice, rasam powder, and turmeric.
Add mashed dal and 2 cups water. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Heat ghee, add mustard seeds, cumin, garlic, curry leaves, red chillies, and asafoetida.
Pour tadka into rasam. Add salt.
Place vadas in a bowl and pour hot rasam over them. Serve immediately.
notes: A delicious South Indian combo where crispy vadas soak up the tangy, peppery rasam. Best served immediately.

name: Veg Kathi Roll
ingredients: For paratha: 1 cup whole wheat flour, salt, water, 1 tbsp oil
For filling: 1 cup mixed vegetables (capsicum, onion, carrot) julienned
preparation: Knead flour with salt and water into a soft dough. Divide into balls and roll into thin parathas.
Cook parathas on a hot tawa with a little oil until golden on both sides.
For filling: Heat oil, add cumin seeds.
Add julienned vegetables and paneer. Stir-fry on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder, and salt.
Toss well and cook for 2 more minutes.
Spread mint chutney on the paratha.
Place the vegetable filling in the center.
Sprinkle chaat masala and lemon juice.
Roll up tightly and wrap in foil. Serve.
notes: Kolkata-style kathi rolls are a fantastic grab-and-go lunch. The paratha wrapping is what makes it a kathi roll.

name: Paneer Kathi Roll
ingredients: For paratha: 1 cup whole wheat flour, salt, water, 1 tbsp oil
For marinade: 2 tbsp yogurt, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp red chilli powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala
preparation: Marinate paneer strips in yogurt, turmeric, red chilli powder, and garam masala for 15 minutes.
Pan-fry marinated paneer on high heat until charred on edges.
Sauté onion and capsicum strips for 2-3 minutes.
Knead dough, roll into thin parathas, and cook on tawa.
Spread mint chutney on the paratha.
Layer the grilled paneer, onion, and capsicum.
Sprinkle chaat masala and lemon juice.
Roll up tightly and secure with foil.
Serve warm as a complete lunch.
notes: Protein-packed paneer roll with charred edges. Great for a quick lunch or packed meal.

name: Chicken Kathi Roll
ingredients: For paratha: 1 cup whole wheat flour, salt, water, 1 tbsp oil
For marinade: 2 tbsp yogurt, 1 tsp red chilli powder, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tbsp lemon juice
preparation: Marinate chicken strips in yogurt, spices, and lemon juice for 30 minutes.
Pan-fry or grill chicken until cooked through with charred edges.
Slice the chicken into thin strips.
Sauté onion rings for 2 minutes.
Roll out parathas and cook on hot tawa.
Spread green chutney on the paratha.
Place chicken strips and sautéed onions in the center.
Sprinkle chaat masala and lemon juice.
Roll tightly and wrap in foil.
Serve warm.
notes: The original Kolkata kathi roll was made with kebab filling. Chicken version is the most popular across India.

name: Egg Roll Kolkata
ingredients: For paratha: 1 cup whole wheat flour, salt, water, 1 tbsp oil
preparation: Knead flour with water and salt. Roll into thin parathas.
Cook paratha on hot tawa with a little oil.
While the paratha is still on the tawa, crack an egg on top.
Spread the egg on the paratha with a spatula.
Flip the paratha so the egg side cooks on the tawa.
Cook until egg is set and paratha is crispy.
Place on a plate, egg side up.
Add sliced onion rings, green chillies, chaat masala, and red chilli powder.
Drizzle green chutney and lemon juice.
Roll up tightly and serve immediately.
notes: The classic Kolkata street food! Egg is cooked directly on the paratha for the authentic experience.

name: Biryani Bowl Veg
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice cooked with biryani spices
preparation: Cook biryani rice with whole spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf).
Sauté mixed vegetables with biryani masala and yogurt.
Assemble the bowl: Layer biryani rice at the base.
Add sautéed vegetables on one side.
Place raita in a small section.
Drizzle saffron milk over the rice.
Top with crispy fried onions.
Add a dollop of mint chutney.
Garnish with pickled onions.
Serve as a deconstructed biryani bowl.
notes: A modern bowl-style presentation of classic biryani flavors. Great for meal prep and lunch boxes.

name: Rice Bowl Rajma
preparation: Prepare rajma masala (cook rajma with onion-tomato masala and spices).
Cook basmati rice until fluffy.
Assemble the bowl: Place steamed rice at the base.
Ladle rajma masala over the rice.
Add onion rings and green chilli on the side.
Place raita or yogurt in a section.
Add pickle and a squeeze of lemon.
Top with crushed papad for crunch.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve as a complete rajma-chawal bowl.
notes: Classic Punjabi rajma chawal in a convenient bowl format. Perfect for a satisfying lunch.

name: Chole Rice Bowl
ingredients: 1 cup steamed rice or jeera rice
preparation: Prepare chole masala with chickpeas, onion-tomato gravy, and chole masala powder.
Cook rice (plain or jeera rice).
Assemble the bowl: Place rice at the base.
Ladle chole masala on one side of the rice.
Add boondi raita on another side.
Add green chutney and pickle.
Top with sliced onions and lemon wedge.
Sprinkle sev for crunch.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve as a hearty chole-chawal bowl.
notes: A vibrant lunch bowl combining tangy chole with fluffy rice and all the trimmings.

name: Dal Rice Tadka Bowl
preparation: Prepare tadka dal (toor dal with garlic-ghee tempering).
Cook steamed rice.
Assemble the bowl: Place steamed rice at the base.
Pour hot tadka dal over the rice.
Add a dollop of ghee on top.
Add raita and pickle on the sides.
Place onion slices and lemon wedge.
Crush papad and sprinkle on top.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
A simple, comforting everyday dal-chawal bowl.
notes: The most comforting Indian meal in bowl form. A hot ghee tadka on top makes all the difference.

name: Dosa Lunch Plate
ingredients: For dosa batter: 1 cup rice soaked, 1/3 cup urad dal soaked (fermented overnight)
For potato masala: 2 potatoes boiled and mashed, 1 onion chopped, 1 tsp mustard seeds, 8-10 curry leaves, 2 green chillies, 1/4 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp oil
preparation: Soak rice and urad dal separately for 6 hours. Grind together to a smooth batter. Ferment overnight.
For potato masala: Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and cook until soft. Add green chillies and turmeric.
Add mashed potatoes, salt, and mix well.
Heat a flat dosa tawa. Pour a ladleful of batter and spread in a circular motion.
Drizzle oil around edges. Cook until crispy and golden.
Place potato masala filling in the center.
Fold the dosa over the filling.
Serve with coconut chutney and a bowl of sambhar.
notes: The classic South Indian masala dosa plate. Fermentation of batter for 8-10 hours is key to crispy dosas.

name: Uttapam Lunch Plate
preparation: Heat a thick-bottomed tawa or pan.
Pour a ladleful of batter and spread to a thick round (not thin like dosa).
Immediately sprinkle chopped onion, tomato, capsicum, green chillies, and coriander on top.
Press the toppings lightly into the batter.
Drizzle oil around the edges.
Cover and cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes until bottom is golden.
Flip carefully and cook for another minute.
Serve hot with coconut chutney and sambhar.
notes: Uttapam is thicker than dosa and topped with vegetables. A great way to use slightly over-fermented batter.

name: Idli Sambar Plate
ingredients: For idli batter: 1 cup rice soaked, 1/3 cup urad dal soaked (fermented overnight)
For sambhar: 1/2 cup toor dal, mixed vegetables, 2 tbsp sambhar powder, tamarind, 1 tsp mustard seeds, curry leaves
preparation: Soak rice and urad dal separately. Grind together, add salt, and ferment overnight.
Grease idli moulds with oil.
Pour batter into moulds and steam for 10-12 minutes.
Prepare sambhar with toor dal, vegetables, sambhar powder, and tamarind.
Prepare coconut chutney by grinding fresh coconut with green chillies and ginger.
Unmould the soft, fluffy idlis onto a plate.
Serve 4 idlis with a bowl of hot sambhar.
Add coconut chutney and tomato chutney on the side.
Drizzle a little ghee on the idlis if desired.
notes: Idli-sambhar is a light, steamed, and easily digestible lunch. The batter must ferment well for soft, fluffy idlis.

name: Misal Pav Lunch
ingredients: For usal: 1 onion chopped, 1 tomato chopped, 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp misal masala or goda masala, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp red chilli powder
For tarri (spicy gravy): 2 tbsp oil, 1 onion, 2 tomatoes, 2 tsp red chilli powder, 1 tsp garam masala
preparation: Pressure cook mixed sprouts for 3-4 whistles until tender.
For usal: Heat oil, add onion and cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste.
Add tomato, misal masala, turmeric, and red chilli powder. Cook until soft.
Add cooked sprouts and 1/2 cup water. Simmer for 10 minutes.
For tarri: Cook onion and tomatoes with extra red chilli powder until a thin spicy gravy forms.
Assemble: Place usal in a bowl.
Pour hot tarri over it.
Top with chopped onion, coriander, lemon juice, and farsan.
Warm the pav buns with butter.
Serve misal with pav on the side.
notes: A fiery Maharashtrian street food breakfast/lunch. The farsan (crispy toppings) add essential crunch.

name: Pav Bhaji Lunch Light
ingredients: For bhaji: 3 potatoes boiled and mashed, 1/2 cup peas, 1/2 cup cauliflower grated, 1 carrot grated, 1 capsicum chopped
Chopped onion and coriander for garnish
preparation: Boil or pressure cook all vegetables until very soft. Mash well.
Heat oil and butter in a wide pan.
Add onions and cook until golden.
Add tomatoes and cook until completely mushy.
Add pav bhaji masala, turmeric, and salt.
Cook until masala is well blended.
Add all mashed vegetables and mix well.
Add 1/2 cup water and mash everything together on the tawa.
Cook for 10-15 minutes, mashing and stirring.
Slit pav buns and toast on a buttered tawa. Serve bhaji with toasted pav, chopped onion, coriander, and lemon.
notes: Mumbai's iconic street food made lighter with less butter. The key is pav bhaji masala and thorough mashing of vegetables.

name: Vada Pav Light
ingredients: For vada: 3 potatoes boiled and mashed, 2 green chillies chopped, 1 tsp mustard seeds, 8-10 curry leaves, 1/4 tsp turmeric, 1/4 tsp asafoetida, 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste
For batter: 1/2 cup besan, 1/4 tsp turmeric, pinch of baking soda, water, salt
preparation: For potato filling: Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves. Add ginger-garlic paste, asafoetida, turmeric, green chillies, and mashed potatoes. Mix well and cool.
Shape into round balls that fit inside pav.
Make besan batter with water, turmeric, baking soda, and salt.
Dip each potato ball in batter and deep fry until golden and crispy.
Spread dry garlic chutney inside pav.
Add green chutney on the other side.
Place the hot vada inside the pav.
Press down gently.
Serve with fried green chilli on the side.
notes: Mumbai's beloved street food! The dry garlic chutney is essential - made from dried coconut, garlic, and red chillies.

name: Thali Mini North Indian
preparation: Prepare dal tadka with toor dal and garlic-ghee tempering.
Make seasonal vegetable sabzi (aloo gobi, bhindi, or paneer).
Cook steamed rice and make 2 fresh rotis.
Prepare raita with yogurt, cucumber, and cumin.
Slice onion, cucumber, and lemon for salad.
Fry or roast papad.
Arrange all components on a thali plate.
Place rotis, rice, dal, sabzi, raita, pickle, salad, and papad.
A complete balanced North Indian lunch thali.
notes: A balanced mini thali with all food groups represented. Adjust portions based on appetite.

name: Thali Mini South Indian
preparation: Cook steamed rice.
Prepare sambhar with toor dal, vegetables, and sambhar powder.
Make rasam with tomato, tamarind, and rasam powder.
Prepare poriyal - a dry stir-fried vegetable with coconut.
Make kootu with dal and vegetables in coconut paste.
Fry appalam in oil or roast on flame.
Arrange on a banana leaf or thali plate: Rice in center.
Place sambhar, rasam, poriyal, kootu around the rice.
Add curd, pickle, and appalam.
A traditional South Indian thali experience.
notes: A balanced South Indian lunch with rice as the base. Traditionally served on a banana leaf.

name: Thali Mini Gujarati
preparation: Prepare Gujarati dal (sweet-sour-spicy with jaggery and lemon).
Make a seasonal shaak (vegetable curry).
Prepare Gujarati kadhi (sweet yogurt curry).
Cook steamed rice and make fresh rotis or thepla.
Make buttermilk (chaas) with yogurt, water, and cumin.
Fry or roast papad.
Arrange all items on a thali plate.
Place rotis, rice, dal, shaak, kadhi, raita/chaas, pickle, and papad.
The sweet-sour-spicy balance is key to Gujarati cuisine.
notes: Gujarati thali is known for its sweet-sour-spicy flavor balance. Even the dal has a touch of sweetness.

name: Pulao Raita Combo
ingredients: For pulao: 1 cup basmati rice, 1/2 cup mixed vegetables, 1 onion sliced, 1 bay leaf, 4 cloves, 2 cardamom, 1 cinnamon stick, 1 tsp cumin seeds, 1 tbsp ghee, salt
For raita: 1 cup yogurt, 1/2 cucumber grated, 1/4 tsp roasted cumin powder, salt, pinch of red chilli powder
preparation: Soak rice for 20 minutes.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and whole spices.
Add sliced onion and fry until golden.
Add mixed vegetables and sauté for 2-3 minutes.
Add soaked rice and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and salt. Cook on low heat until done.
For raita: Whisk yogurt smooth.
Add grated cucumber, roasted cumin powder, salt, and chilli powder.
Mix well and chill.
Serve pulao with chilled raita on the side.
notes: A simple, aromatic one-pot rice meal. Cool raita balances the warm spiced pulao perfectly.

name: Veg Fried Rice Indian
ingredients: 1/2 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, beans, capsicum, spring onion) finely chopped
preparation: Ensure rice is cooled and grains are separate (day-old rice works best).
Heat oil in a wok on high heat.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies, stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add all chopped vegetables and stir-fry on high heat for 2-3 minutes.
Vegetables should remain crunchy.
Add the cooled rice and toss well.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, pepper, and salt.
Toss everything on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
Garnish with spring onion greens.
Serve hot with manchurian or chilli paneer.
notes: Indo-Chinese style fried rice. Day-old cold rice gives the best results - freshly cooked rice gets mushy.

name: Schezwan Fried Rice Indian
preparation: Heat oil in a wok on high heat.
Add ginger-garlic paste and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add chopped vegetables and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes.
Add schezwan sauce and mix well.
Add the cooled rice and toss well on high heat.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, pepper, and salt.
Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until rice is well coated.
Add spring onions and toss briefly.
Serve hot with manchurian gravy or gobi manchurian.
notes: Spicy Indo-Chinese fried rice. Schezwan sauce is the key - made from dried red chillies and Sichuan peppercorns.

name: Hakka Noodles Indian
preparation: Boil noodles in salted water until al dente. Drain and toss with a little oil.
Heat oil in a wok on high heat.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies, stir-fry briefly.
Add cabbage, carrot, and capsicum. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes.
Add boiled noodles to the wok.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, pepper, and salt.
Toss everything together on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
Add spring onions and toss.
Serve hot as a complete lunch or with manchurian.
notes: Indian-style hakka noodles are a beloved Indo-Chinese dish. Don't overcook the noodles - they should be al dente.

name: Chow Mein Indian Style
preparation: Boil noodles until just done. Drain and toss with oil to prevent sticking.
Heat oil in a wide pan or wok on high heat.
Add ginger-garlic paste and stir-fry briefly.
Add all vegetables and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes on high heat.
Add the noodles to the wok.
Add soy sauce, ketchup, vinegar, chilli sauce, sugar, and salt.
Toss everything well on high heat.
The noodles should get slightly crispy at the edges.
Serve hot with green chilli sauce on the side.
notes: Indian chow mein is sweeter and tangier than Chinese original due to ketchup. A hugely popular street food.

name: Manchurian Rice Bowl
ingredients: For manchurian: 1 cup cauliflower or mixed vegetables grated, 3 tbsp cornflour, 2 tbsp maida, salt, oil for frying
For sauce: 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp vinegar, 1 tbsp red chilli sauce, 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp cornflour in water, 1/4 cup spring onion
1 cup fried rice or steamed rice
preparation: Grate vegetables, mix with cornflour, maida, salt, and a little water to form balls.
Deep fry until golden and crispy. Drain well.
For sauce: Heat oil, add ginger-garlic paste and stir-fry.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, chilli sauce, and 1/2 cup water.
Add cornflour slurry to thicken the sauce.
Add fried vegetable balls and toss in the sauce.
Add spring onions and mix.
Serve the manchurian over fried rice or steamed rice in a bowl.
Garnish with spring onion greens.
notes: The classic Indo-Chinese combo. Fry the balls just before adding to sauce for the best texture.

name: Gobi Manchurian Dry
ingredients: 1 medium cauliflower cut into small florets
For batter: 3 tbsp cornflour, 2 tbsp maida, 1/2 tsp pepper, salt, water
preparation: Cut cauliflower into bite-sized florets.
Make a thick batter with cornflour, maida, pepper, salt, and water.
Dip florets in batter and deep fry until golden crispy. Drain well.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok, add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies.
Add capsicum and stir-fry for a minute.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, and chilli sauce.
Add the fried cauliflower and toss well on high heat.
The sauce should coat the florets without making a gravy.
Add spring onions and toss.
Serve immediately as a starter or side with fried rice.
notes: India's most popular Indo-Chinese dish! Serve immediately while crispy - it gets soggy if left too long.

name: Veg Manchurian Gravy
ingredients: For balls: 1 cup mixed vegetables grated (cabbage, carrot, beans), 3 tbsp cornflour, 2 tbsp maida, salt
For gravy: 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp vinegar, 1 tbsp red chilli sauce, 1 tbsp cornflour in 1 cup water
preparation: Grate vegetables finely and squeeze out excess moisture.
Mix with cornflour, maida, pepper, and salt. Form tight balls.
Deep fry until golden crispy. Drain well.
For gravy: Heat oil, add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, chilli sauce, and 1 cup water.
Bring to a boil. Add cornflour slurry to thicken.
Add salt and pepper. Simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Add the fried balls to the gravy.
Add spring onions.
Serve hot over steamed rice or hakka noodles.
notes: Gravy version is perfect over noodles or rice. Add balls to gravy just before serving to maintain some crunch.

name: Chilli Paneer Dry
ingredients: For batter: 2 tbsp cornflour, 1 tbsp maida, salt, pepper, water
preparation: Cut paneer into cubes. Make a light batter with cornflour, maida, salt, and water.
Dip paneer cubes in batter and shallow fry until golden. Drain.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok on high heat.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies, stir-fry briefly.
Add onion and capsicum cubes, stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, and chilli sauce.
Add the fried paneer cubes and toss on high heat.
Add spring onions and toss well.
Serve immediately as a side dish or starter.
Pairs well with fried rice or hakka noodles.
notes: A popular Indo-Chinese starter. Shallow frying paneer instead of deep frying makes it lighter.

name: Sweet Corn Soup With Bread
ingredients: 1 tbsp cornflour mixed in 2 tbsp water
preparation: Boil corn kernels until soft. Blend half the corn to a puree and keep half whole.
Heat butter, add garlic and onion. Sauté until soft.
Add vegetable stock and the corn puree. Bring to a boil.
Add whole corn kernels, salt, and pepper.
Add cornflour slurry to thicken. Stir well.
Simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Add white pepper for extra flavor.
Toast bread slices and cut into triangles.
Serve hot soup in a bowl with toast on the side.
Garnish with coriander and a dash of pepper.
notes: A light but filling soup-and-bread lunch. Blending half the corn gives thickness while whole kernels add texture.

name: Hot And Sour Soup Rice
ingredients: 1/4 cup mixed vegetables julienned (carrot, cabbage, mushroom, bamboo shoot)
preparation: Heat 1 tsp oil, add ginger-garlic paste.
Add julienned vegetables and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and bring to a boil.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, and chilli sauce.
Add cornflour mixed in water gradually, stirring constantly.
Simmer until soup thickens to desired consistency.
Add black pepper, salt, and sesame oil.
Add spring onion.
Serve the hot and sour soup in a bowl alongside steamed rice.
Eat rice with the soup for a complete meal.
notes: Indo-Chinese hot and sour soup with rice makes a light, warming lunch. Adjust vinegar and chilli to taste.

name: Tomato Soup Rice Bowl
preparation: Heat butter, add garlic, onion, and carrot. Sauté until soft.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook for 10 minutes until very soft.
Blend to a smooth puree.
Strain through a sieve for smooth texture (optional).
Return to pot, add 1 cup water or stock.
Add pepper, oregano, and salt. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Add cream or milk and stir.
Serve hot soup in a bowl alongside steamed rice.
Top with croutons and fresh herbs.
Eat the soup with rice for a comforting meal.
notes: Creamy tomato soup paired with rice is a unique but satisfying lunch combination popular in parts of India.

name: Spring Roll Lunch
preparation: Heat oil, add ginger-garlic paste.
Add all vegetables and stir-fry on high heat for 2-3 minutes.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, pepper, and salt.
Cook for 1 minute. Let the filling cool completely.
Place filling on a spring roll wrapper.
Fold the sides in and roll tightly. Seal edges with a flour-water paste.
Deep fry in medium-hot oil until golden and crispy.
Drain on paper towels.
Serve hot with sweet chilli sauce.
Makes a great lunch with a light soup on the side.
notes: Crispy spring rolls with crunchy vegetable filling. Ensure filling is cool before wrapping to prevent soggy rolls.

name: Momos Veg
ingredients: For dough: 1 cup maida (all-purpose flour), water, salt
For filling: 1 cup cabbage finely chopped, 1/4 cup carrot grated, 1/4 cup spring onion chopped, 1/4 cup paneer crumbled (optional)
For chutney: 4-5 dried red chillies, 2 tomatoes, 3-4 garlic cloves, 1 tsp vinegar, salt
preparation: Knead flour with water and salt into a smooth dough. Rest for 30 minutes.
For filling: Mix all chopped vegetables, paneer, ginger-garlic paste, soy sauce, pepper, and salt.
Roll dough into thin small circles.
Place a spoonful of filling in the center.
Pleat and seal the edges by pinching together.
Steam the momos in a steamer for 10-12 minutes until translucent.
For chutney: Roast tomatoes, red chillies, and garlic. Blend with vinegar and salt.
Serve steamed momos with the spicy red chutney.
notes: Steamed momos are a healthy lunch option. The spicy red chutney is essential. Can also be pan-fried for extra crunch.

name: Momos Chicken
ingredients: For dough: 1 cup maida, water, salt
For filling: 250g chicken mince, 1/2 cup cabbage finely chopped, 1/4 cup onion finely chopped, 1/4 cup spring onion
For chutney: 4-5 dried red chillies, 2 tomatoes, 3-4 garlic cloves, 1 tsp vinegar, salt
preparation: Knead dough and rest for 30 minutes.
Mix chicken mince with all vegetables, ginger-garlic paste, soy sauce, pepper, oil, and salt.
Roll dough into thin circles.
Place filling in center and pleat-seal the edges.
Arrange in a greased steamer basket with gaps between momos.
Steam for 12-15 minutes until the wrapper is translucent and chicken is cooked.
For chutney: Roast and blend tomatoes, chillies, and garlic with vinegar.
Serve hot steamed chicken momos with the red chutney.
notes: Chicken momos are juicier than veg ones. Don't overstuff or they'll burst while steaming.

name: Fish Fry Meal
ingredients: For marinade: 1 tsp red chilli powder, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp lemon juice, salt
Rice flour or semolina for coating
For serving: steamed rice, dal, lemon wedges, onion rings
preparation: Clean fish fillets and pat dry.
Marinate with red chilli powder, turmeric, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, and salt.
Let it marinate for 30 minutes.
Coat the marinated fish with rice flour or semolina.
Heat oil in a pan for shallow frying.
Fry fish on medium heat for 3-4 minutes on each side until golden and crispy.
Drain on paper towels.
Serve with steamed rice, a bowl of dal, lemon wedges, and onion rings.
A simple, protein-rich South Indian-style fish meal.
servingSize: 2 fish fillets with rice and dal
notes: Shallow frying keeps it lighter than deep frying. Rice flour coating gives extra crispiness.

name: Prawn Curry Rice
preparation: Clean prawns, devein, and wash well. Marinate with turmeric and salt.
Heat coconut oil, add mustard seeds and fenugreek seeds.
When they splutter, add curry leaves and onion.
Cook until onion is soft.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook briefly.
Add tomato, red chilli powder, and remaining turmeric. Cook until soft.
Add coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add prawns and cook for 5-7 minutes until prawns turn pink and curl.
Do not overcook prawns or they become rubbery.
Serve with steamed rice.
notes: Prawns cook in just 5-7 minutes. Overcooking makes them tough. Kerala-style coconut prawn curry.

name: Crab Masala Light
preparation: Clean crabs thoroughly. Remove the top shell, gills, and clean well.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add onions and cook until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, red chilli powder, and coriander powder.
Cook until masala thickens.
Add crab pieces and coat with the masala.
Add 1/2 cup water and coconut milk.
Cover and cook for 15-18 minutes until crab shells turn red.
Add garam masala and coriander. Serve with rice or appam.
notes: Crab masala needs thorough cleaning before cooking. The meat absorbs all the spicy coconut gravy flavors.

name: Chicken Fried Rice Indian
preparation: Season chicken with salt, pepper, and a little soy sauce.
Heat oil, add chicken and cook until golden and done. Set aside.
In the same wok, add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies.
Scramble eggs if using, push to the side.
Add vegetables and stir-fry for 2 minutes on high heat.
Add cooled rice and toss well.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, pepper, and salt.
Add cooked chicken and toss everything on high heat.
Add spring onions and mix.
Serve hot with manchurian or chilli sauce.
notes: Cook chicken separately first for the best texture. Use day-old rice for the perfect fried rice.

name: Egg Fried Rice Indian
preparation: Beat eggs with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a wok on high heat.
Pour eggs and scramble them. Break into small pieces. Set aside.
In the same wok, add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies.
Add vegetables and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add cooled rice and toss well.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, pepper, and salt.
Add the scrambled eggs back in and mix.
Add spring onions and toss.
Serve hot with chilli sauce or manchurian.
notes: Quick and protein-rich. Scramble eggs first and add them back at the end to keep them fluffy.

name: Paneer Fried Rice
preparation: Cut paneer into small cubes.
Heat oil in a wok, pan-fry paneer until golden on all sides. Set aside.
In the same wok, add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies.
Add vegetables and stir-fry for 2 minutes on high heat.
Add cooled rice and toss.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, pepper, and salt.
Toss on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
Add fried paneer cubes and spring onions.
Mix gently to avoid breaking paneer.
Serve hot with manchurian or chilli sauce.
notes: Pan-fried paneer cubes add a nice golden crust and extra protein to the fried rice.

name: Tandoori Roti Sabzi
ingredients: For tandoori roti: 2 cups whole wheat flour, 1/4 cup yogurt, 1 tsp salt, water

For sabzi: 2 cups mixed vegetables, 1 onion chopped, 2 tomatoes pureed, 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp cumin seeds, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1/2 tsp red chilli powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, 1 tbsp oil
preparation: Mix flour, yogurt, salt, and water to form a stiff dough. Rest for 30 minutes.
Roll into thick rounds (thicker than regular roti).
Cook on a very hot tawa, then place directly on open flame to puff and char slightly.
For sabzi: Heat oil, add cumin seeds and onion.
Add ginger-garlic paste, then tomato puree with all spice powders.
Cook masala until oil separates.
Add vegetables and cook until tender.
Add garam masala.
Serve 3 tandoori rotis with a generous bowl of sabzi.
notes: Tandoori roti is thicker and chewier than regular roti. The yogurt in dough adds softness and flavor.

name: Naan Curry Light
ingredients: For naan: 1 cup maida, 1/4 cup yogurt, 1/2 tsp baking powder, 1 tsp sugar, salt, water, 1 tbsp butter
For curry: 200g paneer or vegetables, 2 onions, 2 tomatoes pureed, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp cumin seeds, spice powders, 1 tbsp oil
preparation: Mix flour, yogurt, baking powder, sugar, salt, and water. Knead into soft dough. Rest 2 hours.
For curry: Heat oil, add cumin seeds and onion. Cook until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste, tomato puree, and spice powders.
Cook until masala thickens. Add paneer or vegetables.
Roll naan dough into oval shapes.
Cook on very hot tawa - the naan should puff.
Flip and cook the other side directly on flame for char marks.
Brush with butter.
Serve 2 naans with a bowl of curry.
notes: Light version uses less butter. Tawa naan is a great home alternative to tandoor naan.

name: Kulcha Chole Light
ingredients: For kulcha: 1 cup maida, 1/4 cup yogurt, 1/2 tsp baking soda, salt, water, 1 tbsp butter, 1 tsp nigella seeds (kalonji)
For chole: 1 cup chickpeas soaked, 2 onions, 2 tomatoes, 2 tsp chole masala, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, whole spices, 1 tbsp oil
preparation: Soak chickpeas overnight. Pressure cook until soft.
Mix kulcha dough ingredients. Knead and rest for 2 hours.
For chole: Cook onions until brown, add ginger-garlic paste.
Add tomatoes, chole masala, and spice powders. Cook until thick.
Add chickpeas and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Roll kulcha dough into rounds, press nigella seeds on top.
Cook on hot tawa with butter until golden on both sides.
Serve 2 kulchas with a bowl of chole.
Add pickled onion and green chutney on the side.
notes: Amritsari kulcha-chole is an iconic Punjabi lunch. Nigella seeds on kulcha are the signature touch.

name: Stuffed Capsicum
ingredients: For stuffing: 2 potatoes boiled and mashed, 1/4 cup paneer crumbled, 1 onion finely chopped, 2 green chillies chopped
preparation: Cut the tops off capsicums and scoop out seeds.
Mix mashed potato, crumbled paneer, onion, green chillies, and all spices.
Stuff the capsicums with the filling and replace the tops.
Heat oil in a pan that fits the capsicums snugly.
Place stuffed capsicums upright in the pan.
Cover and cook on low heat for 15-20 minutes, turning occasionally.
The capsicums should soften but hold their shape.
Remove and serve with roti and raita.
notes: Choose capsicums that can stand upright. Slow cooking on low heat is key to even cooking.

name: Stuffed Tomato
ingredients: For stuffing: 2 potatoes boiled and mashed, 2 tbsp peanuts roasted and crushed, 1 tsp sesame seeds, 2 green chillies chopped
preparation: Choose firm, round tomatoes. Cut tops off and scoop out pulp.
Mix mashed potatoes, crushed peanuts, sesame seeds, green chillies, and all spices.
Add a little tomato pulp to the filling and mix.
Stuff the tomatoes with the filling.
Heat oil in a pan, place stuffed tomatoes carefully.
Cover and cook on very low heat for 10-12 minutes.
Handle gently as tomatoes soften quickly.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve with roti or as a side dish.
notes: A Maharashtrian specialty. Use firm tomatoes that won't collapse while cooking. Peanuts add great crunch.

name: Stuffed Paratha Lunch
ingredients: For dough: 2 cups whole wheat flour, water, salt
For stuffing: 2 potatoes boiled and mashed (or paneer/gobi)
preparation: Knead flour with water and salt into soft dough. Rest for 20 minutes.
Mix mashed potatoes with green chillies, cumin powder, amchur, garam masala, coriander, and salt.
Divide dough into balls. Roll each into a small circle.
Place filling in center, seal edges, and roll out gently into a flat paratha.
Heat a tawa, place the paratha.
Cook on medium heat, flip when brown spots appear.
Apply ghee on both sides and cook until golden and crispy.
Serve hot with yogurt, pickle, and butter.
notes: The quintessential North Indian lunch. Aloo paratha is most popular, but gobi and paneer versions are equally delicious.

name: Veg Pulao Light
ingredients: 1/2 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, peas, beans, cauliflower)
preparation: Soak rice for 20-30 minutes.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and whole spices.
Add sliced onion and fry until golden.
Add green chillies and vegetables, sauté for 3 minutes.
Add soaked rice and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add 2 cups water, mint leaves, and salt.
Bring to a boil, then cook on low heat until done.
Let it rest for 5 minutes covered.
Fluff with a fork.
Serve with raita and pickle.
notes: A simple, fragrant one-pot rice dish. Light version uses less ghee. Perfect for a quick lunch.

name: Matar Pulao
preparation: Soak rice for 20 minutes.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and whole spices.
Add sliced onion and fry until golden.
Add green peas, green chillies, and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add soaked rice and sauté gently.
Add 2 cups water, mint, and salt.
Bring to a boil, then cook on low heat for 15-18 minutes.
Rest for 5 minutes. Fluff with fork.
Serve with raita or any curry.
notes: Simple peas pulao is a quick and elegant lunch option. Use fresh peas in winter for the sweetest flavor.

name: Kashmiri Pulao
ingredients: 2 tbsp mixed dry fruits (almonds, cashews, raisins)
preparation: Soak rice for 30 minutes.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and whole spices.
Add soaked rice and sauté gently for 2 minutes.
Add 2 cups water, sugar, and salt.
Bring to a boil, then cook on low heat until done.
Meanwhile, lightly fry dry fruits in a little ghee.
When rice is done, drizzle saffron milk on top.
Add fried dry fruits and pomegranate seeds.
Gently mix with a fork.
Serve as a fragrant, mildly sweet pulao.
notes: A fragrant, mildly sweet Kashmiri rice studded with dry fruits and saffron. Perfect for special occasions.

name: Spinach Rice
preparation: Blanch spinach, cool, and blend to a puree.
Soak rice for 20 minutes.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cloves, and cardamom.
Add onion and cook until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies.
Add spinach puree and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add soaked rice and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and salt. Bring to a boil.
Cook on low heat until rice is done.
Add garam masala, fluff with fork. Serve with raita.
notes: A vibrant green rice dish packed with iron from spinach. Blanching preserves the bright green color.

name: Fenugreek Rice
preparation: Wash methi leaves well and chop. Remove tough stems.
Soak rice for 20 minutes.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and cumin seeds.
When they splutter, add curry leaves, asafoetida, and peanuts.
Add onion and cook until soft.
Add methi leaves and sauté for 3-4 minutes until wilted.
Add turmeric, green chillies, and soaked rice.
Add 2 cups water and salt.
Cook on low heat until rice is done.
Serve with raita or kadhi.
notes: Methi adds a unique slightly bitter flavor and is great for digestion and blood sugar control.

name: Curry Leaf Rice
ingredients: 2 tbsp curry leaf powder or 2 sprigs fresh curry leaves
2 tbsp sesame oil or coconut oil
preparation: If using fresh curry leaves, fry until crispy and grind to a powder.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds.
When they splutter, add urad dal, chana dal, and peanuts.
Fry until golden. Add dried red chillies and asafoetida.
Add turmeric and curry leaf powder.
Mix well and cook for 1 minute.
Add cooled rice and toss gently.
Mix until every grain is coated with the curry leaf mixture.
Add salt and adjust seasoning.
Serve with papad and pickle.
notes: A South Indian variety rice that makes great use of curry leaves. Curry leaf powder can be stored for weeks.

name: Pesarattu Lunch
ingredients: 1 cup green moong dal soaked 4-6 hours
For upma stuffing (optional): 1/4 cup rava, 1 tbsp oil, mustard seeds, curry leaves, onion, green chillies
preparation: Soak green moong dal for 4-6 hours (with rice if using).
Grind to a smooth batter with green chillies, ginger, salt, and very little water.
The batter should be thicker than dosa batter.
Heat a dosa tawa, pour batter and spread in a circular motion.
Drizzle oil around edges. Cook until the bottom is golden crispy.
Optionally, place upma filling in the center.
Fold the pesarattu over the filling.
Serve with ginger-green chilli chutney.
A protein-rich Andhra Pradesh specialty.
notes: Pesarattu is a protein-rich green moong dal crepe from Andhra Pradesh. No fermentation needed - just soak and grind.

name: Quinoa Buddha Bowl
preparation: Rinse quinoa and cook in 1 cup water for 15 minutes. Fluff with fork.
Season cooked chickpeas with cumin, salt, and a little oil.
Arrange the bowl: Place quinoa in the center.
Add chickpeas, avocado slices, greens, tomatoes, cucumber, and carrot in sections.
Place a dollop of hummus.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice over everything.
Sprinkle salt, pepper, and sesame seeds.
Mix everything together before eating or eat in sections.
notes: A nutrient-dense, balanced meal bowl. Quinoa is a complete protein with all essential amino acids.

name: Mediterranean Grain Bowl
ingredients: 1/2 cup bulgur wheat or couscous cooked
preparation: Cook bulgur wheat according to package directions. Fluff and cool slightly.
Assemble the bowl: Place cooked grain at the base.
Arrange tomatoes, cucumber, olives, and greens around the grain.
Crumble feta cheese on top.
Add a dollop of hummus.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice.
Sprinkle oregano, salt, and pepper.
Garnish with fresh parsley.
A light, refreshing Mediterranean lunch.
notes: Mediterranean diet is one of the healthiest in the world. This bowl captures its essence with whole grains and fresh vegetables.

name: Falafel Bowl
ingredients: For falafel: 1 cup chickpeas soaked overnight (not cooked), 1/2 onion, 3 garlic cloves, 1/2 cup fresh parsley, 1 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp coriander powder, 2 tbsp chickpea flour
For bowl: 1/2 cup cooked rice or quinoa, mixed greens, cucumber, tomato, pickled onion
Tahini dressing: 2 tbsp tahini, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tbsp water, salt
preparation: Blend soaked chickpeas (not cooked!) with onion, garlic, parsley, cumin, coriander, and chickpea flour.
Shape into small patties or balls.
Deep fry or pan-fry until deep golden brown and crispy.
For tahini dressing: Whisk tahini with lemon juice, water, and salt.
Assemble the bowl: Place rice or quinoa at the base.
Add falafel patties (4-5 pieces).
Add mixed greens, cucumber, tomato, and pickled onion.
Drizzle tahini dressing generously.
Serve with warm pita on the side if desired.
notes: Important: Use soaked, uncooked chickpeas for falafel - cooked chickpeas will fall apart. Crispy outside, fluffy inside.

name: Hummus Veggie Wrap
preparation: Warm the tortillas slightly on a tawa.
Spread 2 tbsp hummus evenly on each tortilla.
Layer mixed greens, cucumber, carrot, capsicum, and tomatoes.
Crumble feta cheese on top if using.
Drizzle olive oil and sprinkle herbs, salt, and pepper.
Fold the bottom edge up, then roll tightly from one side.
Cut diagonally in half.
Serve immediately or wrap in foil for a packed lunch.
notes: A fresh, no-cook lunch option packed with plant protein from hummus. Great for meal prep.

name: Grilled Chicken Salad
ingredients: For marinade: 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp paprika, salt, pepper, 1 tbsp lemon juice
For dressing: 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp mustard, salt, pepper
preparation: Marinate chicken breast in olive oil, garlic powder, paprika, salt, pepper, and lemon juice for 30 minutes.
Grill or pan-sear chicken on medium-high heat for 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through.
Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes, then slice.
Toss salad greens, tomatoes, cucumber, and corn in a large bowl.
Whisk dressing ingredients together.
Top the salad with sliced grilled chicken.
Add croutons and drizzle dressing.
Toss gently and serve immediately.
notes: A high-protein, low-carb lunch option. Let chicken rest after grilling for juicier slices.

name: Turkey Club Wrap Light
ingredients: 2 tbsp light mayonnaise or Greek yogurt
preparation: If using turkey bacon, cook until crispy.
Warm tortillas slightly on a tawa.
Spread mayonnaise or yogurt and mustard on each tortilla.
Layer lettuce leaves, sliced turkey breast, and tomato.
Add avocado slices and turkey bacon if using.
Season with salt and pepper.
Roll tightly, tucking in the sides.
Cut diagonally and serve.
A high-protein, lean lunch wrap.
notes: Turkey breast is one of the leanest protein sources. Light version uses Greek yogurt instead of mayo.

name: Tuna Salad Sandwich Light
preparation: Drain tuna thoroughly and flake with a fork.
Mix tuna with Greek yogurt, mustard, celery, red onion, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Toast the bread slices.
Place lettuce on one slice of toast.
Spread tuna salad mixture on top.
Add tomato slices.
Top with another slice of toast.
Cut diagonally and serve.
A high-protein, omega-3 rich lunch.
notes: Using Greek yogurt instead of mayo cuts calories significantly while adding protein. Tuna is rich in omega-3 fatty acids.

name: Poke Bowl
ingredients: 150g sushi-grade raw fish (tuna or salmon) cubed
preparation: Cook sushi rice and season with rice vinegar.
Marinate fish cubes in soy sauce and sesame oil for 10 minutes.
Assemble the bowl: Place sushi rice at the base.
Arrange marinated fish cubes, avocado, edamame, and cucumber.
Add pickled ginger on the side.
Drizzle remaining marinade over the bowl.
Top with sesame seeds and nori strips.
Add sriracha mayo if desired.
Eat immediately while fish is fresh.
notes: Use only sushi-grade fish for raw poke bowls. Rich in omega-3 fatty acids and protein.

name: Sushi Bowl
preparation: Cook sushi rice and season with rice vinegar and a pinch of sugar.
Slice avocado, cucumber, and carrot into thin strips.
Shell edamame.
Assemble the bowl: Place seasoned rice at the base.
Arrange all vegetables in sections on top.
Add pickled ginger and shredded nori.
Drizzle soy sauce and sesame oil.
Add a small amount of wasabi if desired.
Top with sesame seeds and Japanese mayo.
Mix before eating for the best flavor.
notes: A vegetarian deconstructed sushi experience. All the flavors of sushi without the rolling.

name: Teriyaki Chicken Bowl
ingredients: For teriyaki: 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp mirin, 1 tbsp honey, 1 tsp ginger grated, 1 tsp garlic minced
preparation: Mix soy sauce, mirin, honey, ginger, and garlic for teriyaki sauce.
Heat oil, cook chicken pieces until golden on all sides.
Pour teriyaki sauce over the chicken.
Cook until sauce thickens and coats the chicken (3-4 minutes).
Steam broccoli and prepare all vegetables.
Assemble the bowl: Place steamed rice at the base.
Add teriyaki chicken, broccoli, carrot, and edamame.
Drizzle remaining teriyaki sauce over the bowl.
Sprinkle sesame seeds and spring onion.
Serve warm.
notes: Chicken thighs are juicier than breast for teriyaki. The sauce should be glossy and coat the chicken well.

name: Pad Thai Light
ingredients: For sauce: 2 tbsp tamarind paste, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp fish sauce (or soy sauce for veg), 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp red chilli flakes
preparation: Soak rice noodles in warm water for 20 minutes until soft. Drain.
Mix tamarind paste, soy sauce, fish sauce, sugar, and chilli flakes for the pad thai sauce.
Heat oil in a wok. Cook tofu until golden (or cook shrimp until pink).
Push to one side, scramble egg on the other side.
Add soaked noodles and pour the pad thai sauce over.
Toss on high heat for 2-3 minutes.
Add bean sprouts and half the spring onions.
Toss briefly. Transfer to plates.
Top with crushed peanuts, remaining spring onions, and lime wedges.
Serve immediately.
notes: The balance of sweet, sour, salty, and spicy is key to good pad thai. Rice noodles should be soft but not mushy.

name: Green Curry Light
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (eggplant, bamboo shoot, bell pepper, peas)
1 tbsp fish sauce or soy sauce
preparation: Heat oil in a pan, add green curry paste and cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add half the coconut milk and cook until oil separates from the paste.
Add protein (tofu or chicken) and cook for 5 minutes.
Add remaining coconut milk, vegetables, kaffir lime leaves.
Add fish sauce and sugar. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Add Thai basil leaves at the end.
Serve the green curry over steamed jasmine rice.
Garnish with extra basil.
notes: Thai green curry is aromatic and creamy. Use light coconut milk for a lighter version. Thai basil is essential.

name: Tom Kha Gai Light
preparation: Bring chicken stock to a boil. Add galangal, lemongrass, and kaffir lime leaves.
Simmer for 5 minutes to infuse flavors.
Add coconut milk and bring back to a gentle simmer.
Add chicken slices and cook for 5-7 minutes until done.
Add mushrooms and cook for 3 minutes.
Add fish sauce, sugar, and Thai chillies.
Remove from heat and add lime juice (adding lime juice off heat preserves its freshness).
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve as a rich soup with steamed rice on the side.
notes: A creamy Thai coconut soup with aromatic herbs. Galangal and lemongrass are key. Light version uses light coconut milk.

name: Stir Fry Vegetables Rice
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (broccoli, bell pepper, snap peas, baby corn, mushroom)
1 tbsp oyster sauce or hoisin sauce
preparation: Cut all vegetables into uniform pieces.
Heat oil in a wok on high heat.
Add ginger-garlic paste and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add vegetables in order of cooking time (broccoli first, snap peas last).
Stir-fry on high heat for 3-4 minutes. Keep vegetables crisp.
Add soy sauce, oyster sauce, salt, and pepper.
Add cornflour slurry to create a light glaze.
Drizzle sesame oil and add spring onion.
Serve the stir-fried vegetables over steamed rice.
notes: The key to great stir-fry is high heat and not overcrowding the wok. Vegetables should be vibrant and crunchy.

name: Pasta Primavera
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (zucchini, bell pepper, cherry tomato, broccoli, asparagus)
preparation: Cook pasta in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water.
Heat olive oil, add garlic and sauté until fragrant.
Add vegetables and sauté for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Add Italian herbs and chilli flakes.
Add cooked pasta and toss with the vegetables.
Add reserved pasta water as needed for moisture.
Season with salt and pepper.
Add parmesan cheese and fresh basil.
Toss well and serve immediately.
notes: Primavera means spring - use the freshest seasonal vegetables. Pasta water helps create a silky sauce.

name: Penne Arrabbiata Light
ingredients: 4 ripe tomatoes or 1 can crushed tomatoes
1/2 tsp red chilli flakes (or more to taste)
preparation: Cook penne in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water.
Heat olive oil, add garlic slices and chilli flakes.
Cook until garlic is just golden (don't burn it).
Add crushed or pureed tomatoes, salt, and pepper.
Simmer the sauce for 15-20 minutes until it thickens.
Add cooked penne to the sauce.
Toss well, adding pasta water if needed.
Add fresh basil and toss.
Serve with grated parmesan on top.
notes: Arrabbiata means angry - it's the chilli that gives this pasta its fire. Adjust chilli flakes to your preference.

name: Spaghetti Aglio Olio Light
preparation: Cook spaghetti in well-salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water.
Heat olive oil in a large pan on low heat.
Add sliced garlic and cook very slowly until golden (not brown).
Add chilli flakes and cook for 30 seconds.
Add 1/2 cup pasta water to the garlic oil.
Add cooked spaghetti directly to the pan.
Toss vigorously to create an emulsified sauce.
Add more pasta water if needed.
Season with salt, pepper, and fresh parsley.
Serve with parmesan on top.
notes: The simplest Italian pasta yet one of the best. Low and slow garlic cooking is the key - never let it burn.

name: Whole Wheat Pasta Pesto
ingredients: For pesto: 2 cups fresh basil, 1/4 cup pine nuts or walnuts, 3 garlic cloves, 1/4 cup parmesan cheese, 1/4 cup olive oil, salt
preparation: Cook whole wheat pasta in salted water until al dente. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water.
For pesto: Blend basil, nuts, garlic, parmesan, and olive oil to a paste.
Season pesto with salt.
Drain pasta and return to pot.
Add pesto and toss well.
Add pasta water as needed to loosen the pesto.
Add cherry tomatoes and toss gently.
Serve with extra parmesan on top.
A nutrient-rich, fiber-rich pasta lunch.
notes: Whole wheat pasta adds more fiber and protein. Fresh basil pesto is far superior to store-bought.

name: Burrito Bowl
ingredients: 1/2 cup black beans cooked and seasoned
2 tbsp sour cream or Greek yogurt
preparation: Cook rice with lime juice and chopped coriander.
Season black beans with cumin, salt, and lime juice.
Roast or sauté corn kernels.
Assemble the bowl: Place rice at the base.
Add seasoned black beans and corn.
Top with salsa and guacamole or avocado.
Add shredded lettuce and cheese.
Dollop sour cream or yogurt.
Squeeze lime juice and add hot sauce if desired.
Mix everything together before eating.
notes: All the burrito flavors without the tortilla. Customize toppings to your preference.

name: Chicken Quesadilla Light
preparation: Season shredded chicken with cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Heat a large pan or tawa on medium heat.
Place one tortilla flat on the pan.
Spread half the cheese on the tortilla.
Layer chicken, capsicum, onion, and corn.
Sprinkle remaining cheese on top.
Place the second tortilla on top.
Cook until bottom tortilla is golden and crispy (3-4 minutes).
Flip carefully and cook the other side.
Cut into wedges and serve with salsa and sour cream.
notes: Whole wheat tortillas make it healthier. Cook on medium heat for crispy tortilla and melted cheese.

name: Black Bean Tacos
preparation: Season black beans with cumin, paprika, and salt. Heat in a pan.
Warm tortillas on a tawa or directly on flame.
Mix corn, onion, tomato, coriander, and lime juice for a quick salsa.
Assemble tacos: Place warm tortillas flat.
Spoon seasoned black beans on each tortilla.
Top with the corn salsa.
Add avocado slices.
Drizzle hot sauce to taste.
Fold and eat immediately.
notes: Healthy and fiber-rich plant-based tacos. Black beans provide excellent protein and fiber.

name: Fish Tacos Light
ingredients: For marinade: 1 tbsp lime juice, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp paprika, salt, pepper
For slaw: 1 cup cabbage shredded, 1 tbsp lime juice, 1 tbsp mayo or yogurt, salt
preparation: Marinate fish with lime juice, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Grill or pan-sear fish for 3-4 minutes per side until flaky.
Flake the fish into large pieces.
Mix shredded cabbage with lime juice, mayo, and salt for slaw.
Warm tortillas on a tawa.
Assemble: Place fish pieces on tortillas.
Top with cabbage slaw and avocado.
Add salsa and fresh coriander.
Squeeze lime juice over the top.
Serve immediately.
notes: Light fish tacos with a fresh cabbage slaw. Use firm white fish that holds together when grilled.

name: Greek Salad With Pita
ingredients: For dressing: 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp red wine vinegar, 1/2 tsp oregano, salt, pepper
preparation: Dice cucumber, halve tomatoes, slice onion.
In a large bowl, combine greens, cucumber, tomatoes, onion, and olives.
Add cubed feta cheese on top.
Whisk olive oil, vinegar, oregano, salt, and pepper for dressing.
Drizzle dressing over the salad.
Toss gently (be careful with feta).
Warm pita breads on a tawa or in an oven.
Serve the Greek salad with warm pita on the side.
Can also stuff the salad into pita pockets.
notes: A classic Mediterranean lunch. Use good quality feta and olives for the best flavor.

name: Caesar Wrap Light
ingredients: For light Caesar dressing: 2 tbsp Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp Dijon mustard, 1 garlic clove minced, 1 tbsp parmesan grated, salt, pepper
preparation: Make light Caesar dressing: Mix Greek yogurt, lemon juice, mustard, garlic, parmesan, salt, and pepper.
Chop romaine lettuce.
Toss lettuce with the Caesar dressing.
Add croutons and shaved parmesan.
Warm tortillas on a tawa.
Place dressed salad on each tortilla.
Roll tightly, tucking in the sides.
Cut diagonally.
Serve immediately for best crunch.
notes: Light version uses Greek yogurt instead of traditional egg-based Caesar dressing, significantly cutting calories.

name: Caprese Panini Light
ingredients: 4 slices whole wheat bread or ciabatta
1 tbsp balsamic vinegar or balsamic glaze
preparation: Slice tomato and mozzarella into even rounds.
Layer on bread: mozzarella, tomato slices, and fresh basil.
Drizzle olive oil and balsamic vinegar.
Season with salt and pepper.
Top with the second bread slice.
Butter the outside of both bread slices lightly.
Grill on a panini press or tawa with a weight on top.
Cook for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and cheese melts.
Cut diagonally and serve warm.
notes: Classic Italian flavors - fresh mozzarella, tomato, and basil. Balsamic glaze adds a sweet tang.

name: Minestrone With Bread
preparation: Soak and cook kidney beans until tender.
Heat olive oil, add garlic, onion, carrot, and celery. Sauté for 5 minutes.
Add tomatoes, zucchini, and cabbage. Cook for 3 minutes.
Add vegetable stock and cooked beans.
Bring to a boil, then simmer for 15 minutes.
Add small pasta and cook until al dente (10-12 minutes).
Season with Italian herbs, salt, and pepper.
Serve in bowls with fresh basil and parmesan.
Accompany with crusty bread for dipping.
notes: A hearty Italian vegetable soup that's a meal in itself. Use whatever seasonal vegetables you have available.

name: Ribollita
ingredients: 1/2 cup cannellini beans soaked and cooked
3 cups kale or cavolo nero chopped
preparation: Cook beans until tender. Mash half the beans.
Heat olive oil, add garlic, onion, carrot, and celery. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add tomatoes and rosemary. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add kale and cook until wilted.
Add stock, whole beans, and mashed beans.
Simmer for 20 minutes.
Add torn stale bread and stir in.
Cook for 5 more minutes until bread absorbs the broth.
Drizzle olive oil on top and add parmesan.
Serve warm. Even better reheated the next day.
notes: A Tuscan bread soup that was originally made to use up leftover bread. Ribollita means 'reboiled' - it tastes better reheated.

name: Tuscan Bean Soup Lunch
ingredients: 1 cup white beans (cannellini) soaked and cooked
preparation: Cook beans until very tender. Mash a third of them for thickness.
Heat olive oil, add garlic, onion, and carrot. Sauté until soft.
Add tomatoes, rosemary, and sage. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add stock, whole beans, and mashed beans.
Simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Add spinach or kale and cook until wilted.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve with a generous drizzle of olive oil and parmesan.
Accompany with warm crusty bread.
notes: White beans provide creamy texture and protein. Mashing some beans thickens the soup naturally.

name: Thai Basil Chicken Rice
preparation: Heat oil in a wok on high heat.
Add garlic and chillies, stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add chicken mince and cook until browned, breaking up lumps.
Add soy sauce, oyster sauce, fish sauce, and sugar.
Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until chicken is fully cooked.
Add Thai basil leaves and toss until just wilted.
Fry an egg sunny-side up.
Serve the basil chicken over steamed jasmine rice.
Top with the fried egg.
Break the egg yolk over the rice before eating.
notes: A Thai street food classic called Pad Kra Pao. The fried egg on top is essential. Thai basil is different from Italian basil.

name: Bibimbap
ingredients: For gochujang sauce: 1 tbsp gochujang, 1 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp soy sauce
preparation: Cook rice (slightly sticky is preferred).
Prepare each vegetable separately: Blanch spinach and season with sesame oil and salt. Sauté carrot, zucchini, and mushrooms individually with a touch of oil and salt. Blanch bean sprouts.
Mix gochujang sauce ingredients.
Assemble the bowl: Place hot rice at the base.
Arrange all vegetables in separate sections on top of rice.
Place fried egg in the center.
Add gochujang sauce on the side.
Drizzle sesame oil and sprinkle sesame seeds.
Add kimchi if desired.
Mix everything together thoroughly before eating.
notes: Korean mixed rice bowl. The key is to mix everything thoroughly with the gochujang sauce before eating. 'Bibim' means mixing.

name: Japchae
preparation: Cook glass noodles in boiling water for 6-7 minutes. Drain and cut into shorter lengths.
Blanch spinach, squeeze dry, and season with sesame oil and salt.
Sauté each vegetable separately with a little oil - carrot, onion, mushrooms, capsicum.
In a large bowl, combine noodles with soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, and garlic.
Add all the sautéed vegetables and spinach.
Toss everything together gently.
Sprinkle sesame seeds.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
A popular Korean dish for celebrations and everyday meals alike.
notes: Sweet potato noodles have a unique chewy texture. Cooking each vegetable separately preserves individual flavors and textures.

name: Kimchi Fried Rice
preparation: Chop kimchi into small pieces. Reserve the juice.
Heat oil in a wok, add chopped kimchi and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes.
Add rice and break up any clumps.
Add kimchi juice, gochujang, and soy sauce.
Stir-fry on high heat for 3-4 minutes until rice is well-coated.
Push rice to the side, fry eggs sunny-side up.
Plate the fried rice and top with the fried egg.
Drizzle sesame oil, add spring onions, sesame seeds, and nori strips.
Break the egg yolk into the rice before eating.
notes: Use well-fermented (sour) kimchi for the best flavor. The fried egg on top is essential in Korean cuisine.

name: Shawarma Plate Light
ingredients: 250g chicken thigh or breast sliced thin
For marinade: 2 tbsp yogurt, 1 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1/2 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp garlic paste, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tbsp olive oil, salt
For garlic sauce: 3 tbsp yogurt, 1 garlic clove minced, 1 tbsp lemon juice, salt
preparation: Marinate chicken in yogurt and all spices for at least 1 hour (overnight is best).
Cook marinated chicken on a hot tawa or grill until charred edges and cooked through.
Slice into thin strips.
Make garlic sauce: Mix yogurt, garlic, lemon juice, and salt.
Warm pita bread.
Assemble plate: Arrange chicken strips, sliced vegetables, and pickled turnip.
Drizzle garlic sauce generously.
Serve with warm pita on the side.
Can also be wrapped in pita for a shawarma roll.
notes: Yogurt-based marinade tenderizes the chicken. Charring on high heat gives authentic shawarma flavor.

name: Kebab Plate Light
preparation: Mix mince with grated onion, garlic, parsley, and all spices.
Knead well and shape into elongated kebabs around skewers or into patties.
Grill or pan-cook on medium-high heat for 4-5 minutes per side.
Warm pita bread.
Assemble plate: Place kebabs in the center.
Add hummus, sliced vegetables, and pita around.
Drizzle tahini sauce over the kebabs.
Serve with pickled vegetables on the side.
notes: Grating the onion instead of chopping helps bind the kebabs. Pan-cooking in a grill pan gives good char marks.

name: Kofta Wrap
ingredients: For topping: lettuce, tomato, pickled onion
preparation: Mix mince with onion, garlic, parsley, cumin, cinnamon, salt, and pepper.
Shape into small oval kofta balls.
Heat olive oil and pan-fry koftas on all sides until cooked through (about 8-10 minutes).
Warm the flatbreads.
Make yogurt sauce: Mix yogurt with garlic, lemon juice, and salt.
Spread sauce on flatbread.
Place koftas, lettuce, tomato, and pickled onion.
Drizzle more sauce on top.
Roll up and serve immediately.
notes: Middle Eastern kofta in a wrap makes a satisfying lunch. The cinnamon adds warmth that's characteristic of Levantine cuisine.

name: Couscous Salad
preparation: Pour 1 cup boiling water over couscous. Cover and let sit for 5 minutes.
Fluff with a fork and let cool.
Add diced cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, and chickpeas.
Add chopped parsley and mint.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, cumin, salt, and pepper for dressing.
Pour dressing over the couscous salad and toss.
Crumble feta on top if using.
Serve at room temperature.
Great for meal prep as it holds well.
notes: Couscous is the fastest grain to cook - just add hot water! Fresh herbs are essential for this North African-inspired salad.

name: Tabbouleh Plate
preparation: Soak bulgur in water for 20 minutes. Drain and squeeze dry.
Finely chop parsley - it should be the main ingredient, not the bulgur.
Chop mint, dice tomatoes, and chop spring onion.
Combine bulgur, parsley, mint, tomatoes, and spring onion.
Dress with olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Toss well and let sit for 10 minutes for flavors to meld.
Serve on lettuce leaves with warm pita bread.
Traditional Lebanese tabbouleh is mostly herbs with a little grain.
notes: Authentic tabbouleh uses far more parsley than bulgur. It's essentially a herb salad with a touch of grain.

name: Fattoush Lunch
ingredients: 1 pita bread toasted and broken into pieces
For dressing: 3 tbsp olive oil, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 1 garlic clove minced, 1 tbsp pomegranate molasses (optional), salt
preparation: Toast or fry pita bread pieces until crispy.
Combine greens, cucumber, tomatoes, radish, and onion in a bowl.
Make dressing: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, pomegranate molasses, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing over the salad and toss.
Add toasted pita pieces and toss lightly.
Sprinkle generous amount of sumac over the top.
Add fresh mint.
Serve immediately while pita is still crispy.
notes: A Lebanese bread salad. Sumac's lemony flavor is essential. Add crispy pita just before serving.

name: Lentil Soup With Bread
preparation: Heat olive oil, add onion, carrot, and garlic. Sauté for 5 minutes.
Add cumin, turmeric, and cayenne. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add red lentils and stock. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes until lentils are very soft.
Blend partially for a creamy but slightly chunky texture.
Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice.
Garnish with fresh coriander and a drizzle of olive oil.
Serve with warm crusty bread.
notes: A Middle Eastern/Mediterranean staple. Red lentils cook quickly and create a naturally creamy soup. Lemon juice is essential.

name: Black Bean Soup Bowl
ingredients: 1.5 cups black beans soaked and cooked
For topping: sour cream, avocado, coriander, tortilla chips
preparation: Cook black beans until very tender.
Heat olive oil, sauté onion, garlic, carrot, and celery for 5 minutes.
Add cumin, paprika, and cayenne. Cook for 1 minute.
Add cooked black beans and stock.
Simmer for 20 minutes.
Blend half the soup for a creamy-chunky texture.
Season with salt, pepper, and lime juice.
Serve in bowls topped with sour cream, avocado slices, coriander, and crushed tortilla chips.
notes: Black beans make a naturally thick, hearty soup. Smoked paprika adds a wonderful depth of flavor.

name: Chickpea Stew
preparation: Cook chickpeas until tender.
Heat olive oil, add onion and garlic. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add cumin, paprika, and turmeric. Toast spices for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes.
Add chickpeas and vegetable stock.
Simmer for 20 minutes until flavors meld.
Add spinach and cook until wilted.
Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice.
Garnish with parsley and olive oil drizzle.
Serve with crusty bread for dipping.
notes: A versatile Mediterranean-inspired stew. Chickpeas provide plant protein and make it hearty and satisfying.

name: Grilled Fish Plate
ingredients: 2 fish fillets (tilapia, sea bass, or salmon)
For marinade: 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp garlic paste, 1/2 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1 tbsp lemon juice, salt, pepper, fresh herbs
preparation: Marinate fish fillets in olive oil, garlic, spices, lemon juice, and herbs for 20 minutes.
Heat a grill pan or regular pan on medium-high heat.
Cook fish for 3-4 minutes per side until flaky.
Steam vegetables until tender-crisp.
Cook rice or quinoa.
Plate the grilled fish with steamed vegetables and grain.
Squeeze lemon over everything.
Serve with yogurt dip or tartar sauce on the side.
notes: A clean, protein-rich meal. Don't overcook fish - it should be just flaky and moist inside.

name: Baked Salmon Plate
ingredients: For marinade: 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp honey, 1 tsp ginger grated, 1 garlic clove minced, 1 tsp sesame oil
Side: 1 cup steamed broccoli, 1/2 cup brown rice
preparation: Mix soy sauce, honey, ginger, garlic, and sesame oil for the glaze.
Place salmon fillets on a lined baking tray.
Brush the glaze generously on top.
Bake at 200°C (400°F) for 12-15 minutes until salmon flakes easily.
Cook brown rice and steam broccoli.
Plate the baked salmon with rice and broccoli.
Drizzle remaining glaze over the salmon.
Garnish with sesame seeds and spring onion.
Serve with lemon wedges.
notes: Salmon is rich in omega-3 fatty acids. The honey-soy glaze caramelizes beautifully in the oven.

name: Shrimp Stir Fry
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (bell pepper, snap peas, broccoli, baby corn)
preparation: Clean and devein shrimp. Pat dry.
Heat oil in a wok on high heat.
Add shrimp and cook for 1-2 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
In the same wok, add ginger-garlic paste.
Add vegetables and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes.
Add soy sauce and oyster sauce.
Add cornflour slurry for a light glaze.
Return shrimp to the wok and toss.
Drizzle sesame oil and add spring onion.
Serve over steamed rice.
notes: Shrimp cooks very fast - just 2-3 minutes total. Overcooking makes them rubbery. Remove and add back at the end.

name: Tofu Stir Fry Rice
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (broccoli, bell pepper, mushroom, snap peas)
preparation: Press tofu to remove excess water. Cut into cubes.
Heat oil, pan-fry tofu until golden on all sides. Set aside.
In the same pan, add ginger-garlic paste.
Add vegetables and stir-fry on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
Add soy sauce and hoisin sauce.
Add cornflour slurry to create a glaze.
Return tofu and toss gently.
Drizzle sesame oil.
Serve over steamed rice with spring onion and sesame seeds.
notes: Pressing tofu removes water and helps it crisp up when pan-fried. A great plant-based protein source.

name: Tempeh Bowl
ingredients: For marinade: 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp maple syrup or honey, 1/2 tsp smoked paprika, 1/2 tsp garlic powder
For sauce: 2 tbsp peanut butter, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp lime juice, 1 tsp sriracha, water to thin
preparation: Slice tempeh and marinate in soy sauce, maple syrup, paprika, and garlic powder.
Pan-fry marinated tempeh until golden and slightly crispy on both sides.
Make peanut sauce: Whisk peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, sriracha, and water.
Cook brown rice and shell edamame.
Assemble the bowl: Place rice at the base.
Arrange tempeh, edamame, avocado, and pickled vegetables.
Drizzle peanut sauce generously.
Sprinkle sesame seeds.
A protein-packed plant-based bowl.
notes: Tempeh is a fermented soy product with more protein and fiber than tofu. The peanut sauce ties everything together.

name: Edamame Rice Bowl
ingredients: For dressing: 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp sesame oil, 1/2 tsp ginger grated
preparation: Cook rice and shell edamame.
Prepare all vegetables.
Mix dressing: soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and ginger.
Assemble the bowl: Place rice at the base.
Arrange edamame, carrot, cucumber, cabbage, and avocado.
Drizzle the dressing over everything.
Top with sesame seeds and nori strips.
Mix before eating.
A simple, nutritious plant-based lunch bowl.
notes: Edamame provides complete plant protein. This colorful bowl is as nutritious as it is visually appealing.

name: Chicken Pho
ingredients: For broth: 4 cups chicken stock, 1 onion halved, 2-inch ginger piece, 2 star anise, 1 cinnamon stick, 3 cloves, 1 tbsp fish sauce, 1 tsp sugar
For serving: bean sprouts, fresh Thai basil, lime wedges, hoisin sauce, sriracha, sliced chillies
preparation: Char onion and ginger under a broiler or on direct flame until blackened.
Add stock, charred onion, ginger, star anise, cinnamon, and cloves to a pot.
Simmer for 30 minutes. Strain the broth.
Poach chicken in the broth for 15 minutes. Remove and slice thin.
Add fish sauce and sugar to the broth. Adjust seasoning.
Cook rice noodles according to package directions.
Place noodles in a bowl, arrange sliced chicken on top.
Pour hot broth over the noodles and chicken.
Serve with bean sprouts, Thai basil, lime, hoisin, and sriracha.
Each diner customizes their bowl with toppings.
notes: The aromatic broth is the soul of pho. Charring the onion and ginger adds depth. Fresh herbs and lime are essential.

name: Vietnamese Spring Rolls
ingredients: For dipping sauce: 2 tbsp hoisin sauce, 1 tbsp peanut butter, 1 tsp sriracha, water to thin, crushed peanuts
preparation: Cook rice vermicelli and cool.
Prepare all vegetables and herbs.
Dip one rice paper in warm water for 10-15 seconds until pliable.
Place on a flat surface. Layer lettuce, vermicelli, vegetables, and herbs.
Fold the bottom edge up, fold sides in, and roll tightly.
Repeat with remaining wrappers.
Make dipping sauce: Mix hoisin, peanut butter, sriracha, and water.
Top sauce with crushed peanuts.
Serve fresh rolls with the dipping sauce.
Eat immediately or wrap in damp towel to prevent drying.
notes: Fresh (not fried) Vietnamese spring rolls are light and healthy. Don't over-soak the rice paper or it'll tear.

name: Banh Mi Light
ingredients: For pickled vegetables: 1/2 cup carrot julienned, 1/2 cup daikon julienned, 2 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp sugar, salt
preparation: Quick pickle: Mix carrot and daikon with rice vinegar, sugar, and salt. Let sit 30 minutes.
Grill or pan-cook chicken/tofu with soy sauce.
Slice the baguette lengthwise.
Toast lightly.
Spread light mayo on one side.
Layer grilled chicken or tofu.
Add pickled vegetables, cucumber, coriander, and jalapeño.
Drizzle sriracha.
Close and press lightly.
Cut in half and serve.
notes: Vietnamese-French fusion sandwich. The quick-pickled vegetables add essential crunch and tang.

name: Moroccan Tagine Light
preparation: Heat olive oil, add onion and garlic. Sauté until soft.
Add all spices and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add sweet potato, carrot, and cook for 3 minutes.
Add tomato sauce, stock, chickpeas, and dried apricots.
Bring to a boil, then simmer covered for 25-30 minutes.
Vegetables should be very tender.
Meanwhile, prepare couscous.
Serve the tagine over fluffy couscous.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
notes: Moroccan tagine combines sweet and savory beautifully. Cinnamon and apricots are signature Moroccan flavors.

name: Ethiopian Injera Plate
ingredients: For injera (simplified): 1 cup teff flour (or mix of teff and wheat flour), 1.5 cups water, pinch of salt, pinch of baking powder
For lentil wat: 1 cup red lentils, 1 onion diced, 2 garlic cloves, 1 tsp berbere spice, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp oil
For cabbage stir-fry: 2 cups cabbage shredded, 1 carrot, 1/2 tsp turmeric, salt
preparation: For quick injera: Mix teff flour with water to a thin batter. Let rest 30 minutes. Add salt and baking powder. Cook like a large crepe on a hot pan (one side only).
For lentil wat: Sauté onion until very soft. Add garlic, berbere, and turmeric. Add lentils and 2 cups water. Simmer 25 minutes.
For cabbage: Stir-fry shredded cabbage and carrot with turmeric and salt until tender-crisp.
Lay the injera on a large plate.
Spoon the lentil wat and cabbage stir-fry on top of the injera.
Tear pieces of injera and use to scoop up the dishes.
No utensils needed - eat with your hands using injera.
notes: Traditional Ethiopian dining uses injera as both plate and utensil. Teff is gluten-free and nutritious.

name: Jollof Rice Light
preparation: Blend tomatoes, red pepper, and half onion into a smooth paste.
Heat oil, fry sliced onion until golden.
Add the blended tomato-pepper paste.
Cook on medium-high heat for 15-20 minutes until the paste reduces and oil rises to the top.
Add garlic, ginger, thyme, curry powder, paprika, and bay leaf.
Add rice and stir to coat with the sauce.
Add stock. The liquid should be about 1 inch above the rice.
Cover tightly and cook on low heat for 25-30 minutes.
Do not stir while cooking. Rice at the bottom should get slightly charred (party jollof).
Fluff and serve with fried plantains or a side salad.
notes: West Africa's beloved one-pot rice dish. The slightly charred bottom (called party jollof) is the most prized part!

name: Stuffed Bell Peppers
ingredients: For filling: 1/2 cup rice cooked, 1/2 cup black beans cooked, 1/4 cup corn, 1/4 cup onion diced, 1/2 cup tomato sauce
preparation: Cut tops off bell peppers and remove seeds.
Mix cooked rice, black beans, corn, onion, half the tomato sauce, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Stuff the peppers with the rice-bean mixture.
Place in a baking dish. Pour remaining tomato sauce around the base.
Cover with foil and bake at 190°C (375°F) for 30 minutes.
Remove foil, top with shredded cheese.
Bake uncovered for 10 more minutes until cheese melts.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve with a side salad.
notes: Colorful and nutritious baked stuffed peppers. Mix of colors makes a beautiful presentation.

name: Zucchini Boats
ingredients: For filling: 1/2 cup quinoa cooked, 1/4 cup sun-dried tomatoes chopped, 1/4 cup olives chopped, 2 tbsp pine nuts, 1/4 cup feta cheese crumbled
preparation: Cut zucchini in half lengthwise and scoop out the center to create boats.
Chop the scooped zucchini flesh and sauté briefly.
Mix cooked quinoa, sautéed zucchini, sun-dried tomatoes, olives, pine nuts, feta, herbs, salt, and pepper.
Fill the zucchini boats with the mixture.
Top with shredded mozzarella.
Place on a baking tray and bake at 190°C (375°F) for 20-25 minutes.
Cheese should be melted and golden.
Garnish with fresh basil.
Serve as a light, low-carb lunch.
notes: Low-carb lunch option using zucchini as the vessel. Quinoa provides complete protein for the filling.

name: Eggplant Parmesan Light
preparation: Salt eggplant slices and let sit for 20 minutes to draw out moisture. Pat dry.
Dredge in flour, dip in beaten egg, coat with breadcrumbs.
Instead of deep frying, bake breaded slices at 200°C (400°F) for 15 minutes until golden.
In a baking dish, spread some marinara sauce on the bottom.
Layer baked eggplant, marinara sauce, mozzarella, and parmesan.
Repeat layers.
Bake at 190°C (375°F) for 20 minutes until cheese is bubbly.
Let rest for 5 minutes.
Garnish with fresh basil.
Serve with a side salad or garlic bread.
notes: Baking instead of frying makes this significantly lighter. Salting eggplant removes bitterness and excess moisture.

name: Chicken Caesar Bowl
ingredients: For dressing: 2 tbsp Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp Dijon mustard, 1 garlic clove minced, 1 tbsp parmesan, 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, pepper
preparation: Season chicken with salt, pepper, and a little olive oil.
Grill or pan-sear for 5-6 minutes per side. Rest and slice.
Make light Caesar dressing: Blend yogurt, lemon juice, mustard, garlic, parmesan, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Chop romaine lettuce.
Toss lettuce with the dressing.
Arrange dressed lettuce in a bowl.
Top with sliced grilled chicken.
Add shaved parmesan and croutons.
Drizzle extra dressing if desired.
