notes: Light version uses Greek yogurt dressing instead of traditional egg-mayo based. High protein, low carb.

name: Southwest Chicken Bowl
ingredients: For seasoning: 1 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, salt, pepper
preparation: Season chicken with cumin, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Grill or pan-sear chicken until cooked through. Slice.
Warm black beans with a pinch of cumin.
Assemble the bowl: Place rice at the base.
Add chicken slices, black beans, and corn.
Top with salsa, avocado, lettuce, and sour cream.
Squeeze lime juice over everything.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Mix and enjoy a Tex-Mex inspired lunch.
notes: A Tex-Mex inspired protein bowl. Customize toppings to your preference. Great for meal prep.

name: Hawaiian Chicken Bowl
ingredients: For teriyaki glaze: 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp honey, 1 tsp ginger, 1 tsp garlic
preparation: Mix soy sauce, honey, ginger, and garlic for teriyaki glaze.
Cook chicken cubes in a pan until golden.
Add teriyaki glaze and pineapple chunks. Cook until glaze thickens.
Cook steamed rice.
Assemble the bowl: Place rice at the base.
Add teriyaki chicken with pineapple.
Arrange red cabbage, cucumber, and avocado.
Drizzle spicy mayo.
Sprinkle sesame seeds and spring onion.
A tropical-flavored protein bowl.
notes: Sweet and savory Hawaiian-inspired bowl. Pineapple caramelizes beautifully with the teriyaki glaze.

name: Mushroom Risotto Light
preparation: Keep stock warm in a separate pot.
Heat olive oil and half the butter. Sauté mushrooms until golden. Set aside.
In the same pan, add remaining butter and onion. Cook until soft.
Add garlic and cook briefly.
Add arborio rice and toast for 2 minutes.
Add wine if using, stir until absorbed.
Add warm stock one ladle at a time, stirring constantly.
Wait until each addition is absorbed before adding more.
Continue for 18-20 minutes until rice is creamy and al dente.
Fold in mushrooms, parmesan, and thyme. Serve immediately.
notes: Risotto demands patience and constant stirring. The rice releases starch to create the signature creamy texture.

name: Barley Risotto
preparation: Keep stock warm. Rinse pearl barley.
Heat olive oil, sauté mushrooms until golden. Set aside.
Add butter and onion, cook until soft. Add garlic.
Add barley and toast for 2 minutes.
Add warm stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently.
Barley takes longer than arborio - about 30-35 minutes.
Continue adding stock until barley is tender but chewy.
Fold in mushrooms, spinach, parmesan, and herbs.
The consistency should be creamy but with more bite than regular risotto.
Serve immediately.
notes: Barley risotto is higher in fiber than traditional risotto. The barley retains a pleasant chewiness.

name: Wild Rice Pilaf
preparation: Cook wild rice in 1.5 cups water for 40-45 minutes until grains split open.
Separately, cook basmati rice in stock with butter, thyme, and salt.
Toast pecans or almonds in a dry pan.
Sauté onion and celery in a little butter until soft.
Combine both cooked rices.
Add sautéed onion and celery.
Fold in dried cranberries and toasted nuts.
Season with salt, pepper, and fresh parsley.
Serve as a standalone grain dish or as a side.
notes: Wild rice has a nutty, earthy flavor and is actually a grass seed, not true rice. The cranberries add sweet contrast.

name: Turkey Meatball Sub Light
preparation: Mix ground turkey with breadcrumbs, egg, garlic, Italian herbs, salt, and pepper.
Shape into small meatballs (about 10-12).
Bake meatballs at 190°C (375°F) for 18-20 minutes until cooked through.
Heat marinara sauce in a pan.
Add baked meatballs to the sauce and simmer for 5 minutes.
Split sub rolls and toast lightly.
Fill with meatballs and sauce.
Top with shredded mozzarella.
Broil for 2 minutes to melt cheese.
Garnish with fresh basil and serve.
notes: Turkey meatballs are leaner than beef. Baking instead of frying keeps them lighter. Whole wheat sub adds fiber.

name: Veggie Burger Plate
ingredients: For patty: 1 cup black beans mashed, 1/4 cup oats, 1/4 cup breadcrumbs, 1/4 onion finely diced, 1 garlic clove minced, 1/2 tsp cumin, salt, pepper
Side: baked sweet potato fries or salad
preparation: Mash black beans (leave some chunks for texture).
Mix with oats, breadcrumbs, onion, garlic, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Shape into patties. Refrigerate for 15 minutes to firm up.
Pan-fry or grill patties for 4-5 minutes per side until crispy.
Toast the buns.
Assemble: Bottom bun, lettuce, patty, tomato, onion, avocado, sauce, top bun.
Serve with baked sweet potato fries or a side salad.
A satisfying plant-based burger lunch.
notes: Refrigerating the patties before cooking helps them hold together. Oats act as a binder and add fiber.

name: Lean Beef Wrap
ingredients: For seasoning: 1 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, salt, pepper
2 tbsp Greek yogurt or sour cream
preparation: Season beef strips with cumin, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat oil on high heat, sear beef strips for 2-3 minutes until browned.
Let beef rest for 2 minutes, then slice.
Warm tortillas on a tawa.
Spread Greek yogurt or sour cream on each tortilla.
Add shredded lettuce, beef strips, tomato, and onion.
Add salsa.
Roll tightly, tucking in the sides.
Cut diagonally and serve.
notes: Use lean cuts like sirloin strips. Searing on high heat gives good browning while keeping the meat tender.

name: Greek Gyro Light
ingredients: For marinade: 2 tbsp yogurt, 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp oregano, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1 tbsp lemon juice, salt, pepper
For tzatziki: 1/2 cup Greek yogurt, 1/4 cucumber grated, 1 garlic clove minced, 1 tsp dill, 1 tbsp lemon juice, salt
preparation: Marinate meat in yogurt, oil, and spices for at least 1 hour.
Cook marinated meat on high heat until charred edges.
Make tzatziki: Grate cucumber and squeeze dry. Mix with yogurt, garlic, dill, lemon juice, and salt.
Warm pita breads.
Spread tzatziki on pita.
Add sliced meat, tomato, onion, and lettuce.
Fold the pita around the filling.
Serve immediately with extra tzatziki.
notes: Yogurt marinade tenderizes the meat and adds tang. Tzatziki is essential for authentic Greek flavor.

name: Souvlaki Plate
ingredients: For marinade: 2 tbsp olive oil, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 2 garlic cloves minced, 1 tsp oregano, salt, pepper
Greek salad (tomato, cucumber, onion, olives, feta)
preparation: Marinate meat cubes in olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper for 1-2 hours.
Thread marinated meat onto soaked wooden skewers.
Grill or pan-cook skewers on high heat for 3-4 minutes per side.
Prepare a small Greek salad with tomato, cucumber, onion, olives, and feta.
Warm pita bread.
Plate the skewers with Greek salad, pita, and tzatziki.
Squeeze lemon over the meat.
A complete Greek lunch plate.
notes: Souvlaki is Greek grilled meat on skewers. Simple marinade of olive oil, lemon, and oregano is all you need.

name: Spanakopita Light
preparation: Sauté onion and garlic until soft. Add spinach and cook until wilted. Cool and squeeze dry.
Mix spinach with feta, ricotta, egg, dill, nutmeg, salt, and pepper.
Layer 3 phyllo sheets, brushing each lightly with olive oil.
Spread half the spinach filling.
Layer 3 more phyllo sheets with oil.
Add remaining filling. Fold edges in.
Top with a final brushing of olive oil.
Score the top into portions.
Bake at 190°C (375°F) for 25-30 minutes until golden and crispy.
Let cool slightly before cutting. Serve with a light salad.
notes: Greek spinach and feta pie. Light version uses olive oil brushing instead of butter-soaked phyllo. Still crispy and delicious.

name: Corn Chowder Light
preparation: Heat butter, add onion and garlic. Sauté until soft.
Add diced potato and cook for 2 minutes.
Add corn kernels (reserve some for garnish) and stock.
Bring to a boil, simmer for 15-20 minutes until potato is very soft.
Blend half the soup for a creamy-chunky texture.
Return to pot, add milk and smoked paprika.
Heat through without boiling.
Season with salt, pepper, and thyme.
Serve in bowls topped with reserved corn kernels and a sprinkle of paprika.
Accompany with warm bread.
notes: Light version uses low-fat milk instead of cream. Blending half gives the perfect creamy-chunky texture.

name: Broccoli Cheddar Soup Light
preparation: Heat butter, add onion, garlic, and carrot. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add flour and cook for 1 minute to form a roux.
Gradually add stock, stirring to avoid lumps.
Add broccoli florets and simmer for 15 minutes until very tender.
Blend until smooth (or leave slightly chunky).
Return to pot, add milk and cheese.
Stir until cheese melts. Do not boil.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve in a bread bowl or with crusty bread on the side.
notes: Sharp cheddar gives better flavor with less cheese needed. Light version uses low-fat milk and a flour-based roux.

name: Tomato Basil Soup Lunch
ingredients: Grilled cheese sandwich or crusty bread
preparation: Place tomatoes, onion, and garlic on a baking tray. Drizzle with olive oil.
Roast at 200°C (400°F) for 25-30 minutes until charred.
Transfer roasted vegetables to a pot. Add stock.
Blend until smooth.
Simmer for 10 minutes. Add sugar, salt, and pepper.
Add cream or milk and fresh basil.
Blend once more or tear basil and stir in.
Serve in bowls with a grilled cheese sandwich on the side.
Drizzle olive oil and add basil leaves for garnish.
notes: Roasting the tomatoes intensifies their sweetness and adds depth. Classic comfort food pairing with grilled cheese.

name: Nicoise Salad
ingredients: For dressing: 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp red wine vinegar, 1 tsp Dijon mustard, salt, pepper
preparation: Hard boil eggs, cool, and halve.
Blanch green beans in salted water for 3 minutes. Cool in ice water.
Boil potato until tender, slice.
Arrange salad greens on a large plate.
Arrange tuna (broken into chunks), eggs, green beans, tomatoes, olives, and potato in sections.
Make dressing: Whisk olive oil, vinegar, mustard, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing over the salad.
Serve as a composed salad - do not toss.
A classic French composed salad.
notes: A classic French salad from Nice. It's a composed salad - ingredients are arranged, not tossed. Each bite is curated.

name: Cobb Salad Light
ingredients: 2 strips turkey bacon cooked and crumbled
For dressing: 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp red wine vinegar, 1 tsp Dijon mustard, salt, pepper
preparation: Grill chicken breast and dice.
Hard boil eggs and dice. Cook turkey bacon and crumble.
Chop romaine lettuce and place in a large bowl.
Arrange diced chicken, eggs, bacon, avocado, tomatoes, and blue cheese in neat rows on top.
Make dressing: Whisk olive oil, vinegar, mustard, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing over the salad.
Toss just before eating or eat in sections.
A protein-packed American classic.
notes: Light version uses turkey bacon and a vinaigrette instead of creamy dressing. Very high protein, low carb.

name: Asian Sesame Chicken Salad
ingredients: 2 tbsp crispy wonton strips or almonds
For dressing: 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp honey, 1 tsp ginger grated
preparation: Grill chicken breast, rest, and slice thin.
Shred napa cabbage and carrot.
Make dressing: Whisk soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, honey, and ginger.
Toss cabbage and carrot with the dressing.
Arrange dressed salad in a bowl.
Top with sliced chicken, edamame, and mandarin segments.
Sprinkle crispy wontons or almonds and sesame seeds.
Serve immediately for best crunch.
notes: A refreshing Asian-fusion salad. The sesame dressing ties all the flavors together. Mandarin adds sweet contrast.

name: Miso Ramen Light
ingredients: For broth: 3 cups vegetable or chicken stock, 2 tbsp white miso paste, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp ginger grated, 1 garlic clove minced
preparation: Cook ramen noodles according to package directions. Drain.
For broth: Heat stock. Add ginger and garlic.
Simmer for 5 minutes. Remove from heat.
Dissolve miso paste in a little warm broth, then add back (never boil miso).
Add soy sauce and sesame oil.
Sauté mushrooms until golden.
Soft boil an egg (6-7 minutes for runny yolk). Peel and halve.
Place noodles in a bowl, pour hot miso broth over.
Top with soft-boiled egg, mushrooms, corn, nori, bean sprouts, and spring onion.
Add chilli oil if desired. Serve immediately.
notes: Never boil miso - it kills the beneficial probiotics and changes the flavor. Dissolve off-heat for best results.
